recommendations: Add a real test suite (unit tests for analytics functions in `modules/empyrical`
app/controller) to reduce the current single-file bottleneck
fix packaging metadata detection by correcting and validating `pyproject.toml` (build-system
add explicit dependency files and pin ranges (`pyproject` + optional `requirements.txt`) including dev/test extras
`eigenledger` console script) instead of relying on `python -m ...`
add CI workflows for lint/test/build/publish (not just publish) with matrix testing across Python versions
improve observability and error handling (structured logging
graceful failures for missing market data/APIs)
document architecture and execution paths in README (module responsibilities
add API-level docstrings/type hints across core functions to improve maintainability and MCP toolability
remove or clearly vendor-lock third-party `empyrical` code with attribution/update strategy to avoid drift
add benchmark/performance checks for heavy stats computations (vectorization hotspots
add security and release hygiene (dependency scanning
and add MCP plugin tests plus endpoint documentation to ensure the generated MCP layer remains functional and discoverable

success_factors: Workflow orchestration was resilient: despite git clone timeout, pipeline continued via zip fallback and completed all nodes.
Import-first adapter strategy was appropriate for a Python package and enabled MCP layer generation without deep code rewrites.
Generated MCP service started healthy over stdio, indicating baseline runtime viability.
Risk and complexity were assessed as medium with maintainability index 75, supporting feasible integration.
failure_reasons: Primary repository acquisition failed due to network timeout in DownloadNode (CloneFailed).
DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing enrichment depth for architecture/tool inference.
Static detection pipeline reported zero processed files / no dependencies in one stage, indicating metadata/telemetry inconsistency.
Generated plugin exposed zero concrete endpoints/tools, limiting functional MCP value.

node_performance.download_time: Download encountered clone timeout; recovery via zip fallback succeeded but likely consumed a significant share of total 246.15s.
node_performance.analysis_time: Analysis completed with mixed quality: file tree discovered (17 files), but dependency and entry-point detectors under-reported.
node_performance.generation_time: Generation succeeded quickly enough to produce scaffold files, but output was largely boilerplate with no endpoint surface.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin health check passed (startup healthy), while original project tests were effectively absent (0 test files, no meaningful execution).

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: No reliable metrics recorded (reported 0 MB); efficiency cannot be validated.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: No reliable metrics recorded (reported 0%); CPU behavior is unobservable from current telemetry.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Generated artifact footprint reported as 0 bytes/LOC in summary, conflicting with actual files present; disk accounting instrumentation appears incomplete.
